one_line_definition: A temperature sensor that exploits the predictable change in electrical resistance of a metal (typically platinum) with temperature
A platinum RTD with a nominal resistance of 100 Ω at 0 °C, standardised in EN 60751
A platinum RTD with a nominal resistance of 100 Ω at 0 °C, standardised in EN 60751
A platinum RTD with a nominal resistance of 1000 Ω at 0 °C; preferred for 2-wire applications due to lower lead resistance error
Wiring configuration that compensates lead resistance error; standard for Pt100 industrial use up to ~30 m cable
Wiring configuration that compensates lead resistance error; standard for Pt100 industrial use up to ~30 m cable
Simplest RTD wiring; lead resistance adds directly to measurement error; suitable for Pt1000 or short cables
Fully eliminates lead resistance error; used in laboratory and calibration applications
EN 60751 tolerance class: ±(0.30 + 0.005|t|) °C; standard for most industrial Pt100/Pt1000 sensors
EN 60751 tolerance class: ±(0.30 + 0.005|t|) °C; standard for most industrial Pt100/Pt1000 sensors
EN 60751 tolerance class: ±(0.15 + 0.002|t|) °C; higher precision than Class B
EN 60751 highest standard tolerance class: ±(0.10 + 0.0017|t|) °C
International standard defining electrical resistance versus temperature characteristics for platinum resistance thermometers
Mathematical formula relating Pt100/Pt1000 resistance to temperature using constants A, B, and C
Property of a material whose electrical resistance increases with rising temperature; applies to platinum RTDs
RTD element made by depositing a thin platinum film on a ceramic carrier; standard design for most industrial sensors
RTD element made from a fine platinum wire; available in glass or ceramic-housed variants with wider temperature ranges
Flexible, self-adhesive housing for an RTD sensor enabling surface mounting on pipes and ducts
Perfluoroalkoxy (PFA) insulated lead wire used for RTD connections; resistant to chemicals and suitable for moderate temperatures
Method of measuring temperature on the surface of objects by adhering or clamping a sensor directly to the surface
Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning systems; common application area for surface-mount RTDs
Ability of a thermometer sensor to maintain specification under mechanical vibration; rated in g peak-to-peak per EN 60751
Rugged cable construction using mineral insulation; used to package RTD sensors in 3 mm and 6 mm diameter forms
A thermometer assembly containing two independent measuring resistors for redundancy or differential measurement
